page: Microsoft Azure FX32-8mds_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It features 8 virtual CPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The server is highly memory-optimized, providing 672.0 GB of RAM, which equates to 84.0 GB of memory per physical core. It also includes 1890 GB of local storage and is premium storage capable, with no bundled GPUs.

Benchmark results indicate strong single-core performance, highlighted by a Geekbench single-core score of 2352.0 and a text processing score of 2945.0, both placing in the top tier. Single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression also perform strongly, reaching 34.73 GB/sec and 14.39 GB/sec respectively. Multi-core and memory bandwidth benchmarks generally fall within the average range, with a multi-core Geekbench score of 8736.0 and a large-block read-only memory bandwidth of 71.78 GB/sec. However, LLM inference tasks on larger models show weaker performance, with text generation on a medium 7B model yielding 7.72 tokens/sec.

Given the massive memory-to-CPU ratio of 84.0 GB per core, this server offers high resource density for memory-bound applications. It is qualitatively cost-efficient for workloads requiring vast memory footprints but moderate compute power, avoiding the expense of higher vCPU tiers. The combination of high-speed Intel Xeon cores, substantial local storage, and extreme memory capacity makes this server well-suited for large in-memory databases, memory-intensive data analytics, and enterprise applications requiring significant caching.
description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized x86_64 server featuring 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz. It is equipped with an exceptionally large memory capacity of 672.0 GB (84.0 GB per core) and 1890 GB of local storage, with no GPUs. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core capabilities, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2352.0 and strong single-threaded compression speeds. Conversely, multi-core performance and large-model LLM inference speeds are average to weak. This hardware profile provides high memory density, making it a highly specialized option for memory-intensive workloads. It is best suited for in-memory databases, large-scale caching, and data processing tasks that require massive RAM configurations without requiring a high number of compute cores.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8mds_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 672.0 GB of RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive workloads.

tagline: A memory-intensive server combining high-speed Intel Xeon processing with an exceptional memory-to-core ratio and substantial local storage.

672.0 GB of RAM providing 84.0 GB of memory per physical core
1890 GB of local storage with premium storage capability
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and text processing
Optimized for in-memory databases and memory-intensive enterprise workloads
